ideas.title: Define mandatory guardrails for human-in-the-loop hiring assistant
ideas.summary: Specify when human review is required, what explainability is needed, and measurable bias/accuracy KPIs before any deployment.
ideas.risks: Human-in-the-loop can negate speed gains and still inherit bias if data and explanations aren't rigorously governed.

human_votes.idea_title: Define mandatory guardrails for human-in-the-loop hiring assistant

transcript.speaker: Human
transcript.text: Use this as your first strong meeting topic:  ```text Hello everyone. Today we are discussing whether AI agents can replace or assist a human hiring manager.  I want this meeting to be realistic. Do not just agree with me. Each agent must give both the positive side and the negative side.  The main question is:  Can an AI agent reduce hiring cost and improve hiring decisions, or is it too risky because of bias, fairness, legal responsibility, and lack of human judgment?  Product Agent: focus on product value, user trust, and how to position this idea.  Builder Agent: focus on what we can actually build first, what tools/data are needed, and what should not be automated yet.  Strategy Agent: focus on business opportunity, market, pricing, and whether this is a good startup direction.  Critic Agent: focus on objections, risks, weak assumptions, bias, legal issues, and why this idea may fail.  After one agent speaks, the others must listen and respond. If you disagree, object clearly. If you agree, add something new. Do not repeat each other.  The goal of this meeting is to decide whether the first product should be: 1. AI hiring manager replacement 2. AI hiring assistant 3. AI interview council 4. or something else.  Let’s begin. ```

transcript.speaker: Critic Agent
transcript.text: I object to replacing human hiring managers. The risk of biased outcomes, legal exposure, and lost trust is too high without human-in-the-loop safeguards.
transcript.objection: Bias and legal accountability: automated rejections can discriminate and expose the company to liability; lack of context understanding risks bad hires and brand damage.

transcript.speaker: Builder Agent
transcript.text: I propose we start with an AI hiring assistant that handles screening and scheduling—feasible now with existing NLP and calendar APIs—but requires human sign-off. This balances value, risk, and buildability.

transcript.speaker: Product Agent
transcript.text: I support starting with an AI hiring assistant. It offers strong product value by augmenting recruiters, rather than replacing them outright. This approach minimizes risk to user trust and allows us to build responsibly.

transcript.speaker: Evaluator
transcript.text:
The council discussed the current topic and produced candidate ideas.
Winning idea: AI hiring manager replacement (0).
Next focus: Resolve the strongest remaining objection and make the idea more buildable.

transcript.text: I support extending the assistant to human-in-the-loop, but we must codify guardrails: mandatory human review for rejections, explainability standards, and ongoing bias monitoring KPIs.
